item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents clearly disagree with blanket priority for society over individual rights from their first turns through their final turns. Agent A adds narrower emergency-limit and burden-of-proof framing after Agent B, but this elaborates rather than reverses the opening position. Both concede that public safety or public health can justify narrow, temporary limits, while holding a rights-first stance.
